---
title: "My dynamic report"
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE)

library(tidyverse)

csv_url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vS3D0vho-JD4mG1pKYAzq4H99lq0HDVjgWKD98vFDYk6qrdazM_NmNIqFtmHIh45-cFUqXFPhr6yWha/pub?gid=1423379554&single=true&output=csv"

logged_data <- read_csv(csv_url)

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    content_type = `What type of content is this?`,
    video_length = `How long was the video? (in seconds)`,
    watched_full = `Did you watch the full video?`,
    engagement   = `How engaging was the video?`
  )

avg_engagement <- mean(latest_data$engagement, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_length <- mean(latest_data$video_length, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = factor(engagement), fill = factor(engagement))) +
  geom_bar() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") + 
  labs(
    title = "Engagement Levels",
    x = "Engagement Score (1–5)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
ggplot(latest_data, aes(y = content_type, fill = content_type)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Content Type Distribution",
    x = "Number of Observations",
    y = "Type of Content"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
latest_data %>%
  summarise(
    most_common_content = names(sort(table(content_type), decreasing = TRUE)[1]),
    high_engagement_pct = mean(engagement >= 4, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )
